title: Essential Duas for Daily Life: Morning, Evening, Before Sleep & More
excerpt: The Prophet ﷺ taught us specific duas for nearly every moment of life. This comprehensive guide covers the most important daily duas with Arabic, transliteration, meaning, and the sunnah behind each one.
author: QuranBookk
content:
<HadithBlock
  text="Dua is worship."
  narrator="Nu'man ibn Bashir (رضي الله عنه)"
  source="Sunan Abi Dawud 1479, Sunan al-Tirmidhi 2969 — Authenticated"
/>

And in a profound hadith qudsi, Allah says: *"I am as My servant thinks of Me, and I am with him when he remembers Me."* (Sahih al-Bukhari 7405)

Dua is the most direct line between a Muslim and their Creator. Unlike the five prayers which are fixed in time and form, dua is unlimited — every moment of every day is an invitation to turn to Allah. The Prophet ﷺ gave us specific supplications to sanctify every ordinary moment of life.

## Morning Adhkar (After Fajr)

The morning adhkar are among the most powerful acts of worship. The Prophet ﷺ said: *"Whoever says Subhan Allah 100 times in the morning and 100 times in the evening, no one will come on the Day of Resurrection with anything better than him except one who said more."* (Sahih Muslim)

**Ayat al-Kursi**
Recite once after every obligatory prayer. The Prophet ﷺ said: *"Whoever recites Ayat al-Kursi after every obligatory prayer, nothing prevents him from entering Paradise except death."* (Sunan al-Nasa'i, authenticated by al-Albani)

**The Three Quls**
Recite Surah Al-Ikhlas, Al-Falaq, and An-Nas three times each in the morning and evening. These three surahs together provide comprehensive spiritual protection.

**Morning Dua for Protection**

*بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الَّذِي لا يَضُرُّ مَعَ اسْمِهِ شَيْءٌ فِي الأَرْضِ وَلا فِي السَّمَاءِ وَهُوَ السَّمِيعُ العَلِيمُ*

*Bismillahi-lladhi la yadurru ma'asmihi shay'un fil-ardi wa la fis-sama'i, wa huwas-sami'ul-'alim*

*"In the name of Allah, with Whose name nothing on earth or in heaven can cause harm, and He is the All-Hearing, All-Knowing."*

Recite 3 times. The Prophet ﷺ said whoever says this in the morning will not be afflicted by sudden calamity until the evening.

## Evening Adhkar (After Asr)

Many of the morning adhkar are repeated in the evening. Additionally:

**Sayyid al-Istighfar (Master Supplication of Forgiveness)**

*اللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ رَبِّي لا إِلَهَ إِلا أَنْتَ خَلَقْتَنِي وَأَنَا عَبْدُكَ وَأَنَا عَلَى عَهْدِكَ وَوَعْدِكَ مَا اسْتَطَعْتُ...*

*Allahumma anta rabbi la ilaha illa anta, khalaqtani wa ana 'abduka...*

The Prophet ﷺ said: *"Whoever says this with certainty in the evening and dies that night will enter Paradise, and whoever says it in the morning with certainty and dies that day will enter Paradise."* (Sahih al-Bukhari)

## Before Sleep

**Ayat al-Kursi** — Recite before sleeping. The Prophet ﷺ said a guardian from Allah will protect you until morning.

**Surah Al-Mulk (67)** — The Prophet ﷺ said this surah intercedes for its reciter until they are forgiven.

**The Final Dua Before Sleep**

*اللَّهُمَّ بِاسْمِكَ أَمُوتُ وَأَحْيَا*

*Allahumma bismika amutu wa ahya*

*"O Allah, in Your name I die and I live."*

And upon waking:

*الحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي أَحْيَانَا بَعْدَ مَا أَمَاتَنَا وَإِلَيْهِ النُّشُور*

*"Praise be to Allah Who gave us life after causing us to die, and to Him is the resurrection."*

## Duas for Specific Occasions

**Entering the home**

*اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ خَيْرَ الْمَوْلَجِ وَخَيْرَ الْمَخْرَجِ...*

Begin with Bismillah, greet your family with salam, and recite this dua — shaytan cannot enter a home where Allah's name is mentioned.

**Before eating**

*بِسْمِ اللَّهِ* — "In the name of Allah." If you forget at the beginning, say when you remember: *بِسْمِ اللَّهِ أَوَّلَهُ وَآخِرَهُ* ("In the name of Allah at its beginning and its end.")

**After eating**

*الحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي أَطْعَمَنِي هَذَا وَرَزَقَنِيهِ مِنْ غَيْرِ حَوْلٍ مِنِّي وَلَا قُوَّةٍ*

*"All praise is due to Allah Who fed me this and provided it for me without any strength or power on my part."*

The Prophet ﷺ said the one who recites this is forgiven their previous sins.

**When leaving home**

*بِسْمِ اللَّهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ عَلَى اللَّهِ وَلَا حَوْلَ وَلَا قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِاللَّهِ*

*"In the name of Allah, I trust in Allah; there is no might and no power except with Allah."*

The angel says to the one who recites this: "You are guided, defended, and protected." (Sunan Abi Dawud)

**When hearing bad news**

*إِنَّا لِلَّهِ وَإِنَّا إِلَيْهِ رَاجِعُونَ*

*Inna lillahi wa inna ilayhi raji'un*

*"Indeed we belong to Allah, and indeed to Him we will return."*

**Dua for anxiety and distress**

*اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الهَمِّ وَالحَزَنِ...*

*"O Allah, I seek refuge in You from worry and grief, from incapacity and laziness, from cowardice and miserliness, from being overcome by debt and overpowered by men."*

(Sahih al-Bukhari)

## The Best Times for Dua

<Callout type="tip">
Five times when dua is especially likely to be accepted: the last third of the night (tahajjud), between adhan and iqamah, in prostration (sujood), after obligatory prayers, and on Fridays in the last hour before Maghrib.
</Callout>
